exercises.title: gRPC test approach selection
exercises.instructions:
Choose the best testing approach for each scenario:
A: Unit test business logic in PaymentValidator (no gRPC involved)
B: Test that CreatePayment RPC serializes proto fields correctly
C: Test that auth interceptor rejects missing Bearer token
D: Load test the gRPC server at 10,000 RPCs/second
E: Test that the streaming RPC sends exactly 3 status updates
exercises.expected_solution:
A: Unit test PaymentValidator — PURE UNIT TEST
   Mock or call directly — no gRPC at all
   validator.validate(amount, currency) → result
   Fastest (1ms), tests one class only
   No InProcess server needed

B: Test proto serialization — INPROCESS SERVER
   Real service + real proto serialization/deserialization
   Catches wrong field types, missing required fields
   InProcess: no network overhead (~15ms)
   Blocking stub: synchronous assertion on response fields

C: Test auth interceptor — INPROCESS SERVER with interceptor
   InProcessServerBuilder.addService(impl).intercept(authInterceptor)
   Client sends request without Bearer header
   assertThrows(StatusRuntimeException.class, () -> stub.createPayment(...))
   Check: ex.getStatus().getCode() == UNAUTHENTICATED

D: Load test at 10,000 RPCs/second — REAL NETWORK (staging)
   InProcess can't simulate network latency, connection limits
   Use grpcurl or ghz (gRPC load testing tool) against staging server
   Never load test production gRPC servers

E: Test streaming RPC — INPROCESS SERVER with TestStreamObserver
   AsyncStub + TestStreamObserver captures onNext callbacks
   observer.awaitCompletion() waits for stream to finish
   assertEquals(3, observer.getValues().size())
   Verify each status in order: INITIATED, PROCESSING, CONFIRMED

test_cases.expected:
A: Unit test PaymentValidator — PURE UNIT TEST
   Mock or call directly — no gRPC at all
   validator.validate(amount, currency) → result
   Fastest (1ms), tests one class only
   No InProcess server needed

B: Test proto serialization — INPROCESS SERVER
   Real service + real proto serialization/deserialization
   Catches wrong field types, missing required fields
   InProcess: no network overhead (~15ms)
   Blocking stub: synchronous assertion on response fields

C: Test auth interceptor — INPROCESS SERVER with interceptor
   InProcessServerBuilder.addService(impl).intercept(authInterceptor)
   Client sends request without Bearer header
   assertThrows(StatusRuntimeException.class, () -> stub.createPayment(...))
   Check: ex.getStatus().getCode() == UNAUTHENTICATED

D: Load test at 10,000 RPCs/second — REAL NETWORK (staging)
   InProcess can't simulate network latency, connection limits
   Use grpcurl or ghz (gRPC load testing tool) against staging server
   Never load test production gRPC servers

E: Test streaming RPC — INPROCESS SERVER with TestStreamObserver
   AsyncStub + TestStreamObserver captures onNext callbacks
   observer.awaitCompletion() waits for stream to finish
   assertEquals(3, observer.getValues().size())
   Verify each status in order: INITIATED, PROCESSING, CONFIRMED

hints: InProcess: real gRPC without network — best for integration-level gRPC tests
Pure unit test: no InProcess needed for logic that doesn't touch gRPC
Load test: needs real network + realistic environment

exercises.title: Complete the InProcess server setup
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blanks in the InProcess test setup.

blanks.hint: creates a unique test server name

hints: generateName(): ensures unique server per test class
directExecutor(): single-threaded — eliminates race conditions in tests
@AfterEach: always shutdown to prevent resource leaks

exercises.title: TestStreamObserver implementation
exercises.instructions:
Write TestStreamObserver<T>:
1. Captures all onNext values in a list
2. Records error from onError
3. CountDownLatch: decrements on onCompleted or onError
4. awaitCompletion(timeout): blocks until stream done
5. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): values captured, error recorded, latch released
hints: CopyOnWriteArrayList: thread-safe — gRPC may call onNext from different threads
CountDownLatch(1): release on either onCompleted or onError
awaitCompletion timeout: 5 seconds — prevents hanging CI

exercises.title: Testing unary gRPC RPC with InProcess
exercises.instructions:
Write InProcess test for a simple EchoService:
1. EchoServiceImpl: returns request message with ECHO prefix
2. InProcessServer setup + teardown
3. BlockingStub for synchronous call
4. @SpringBootTest equivalent: 2 tests — happy path, empty message
hints: InProcess test: same logic as calling real stub through network, but in-JVM
INVALID_ARGUMENT: validation failure (wrong input)
assertThrows + getStatus().getCode(): pattern for testing gRPC errors

exercises.title: Testing gRPC status exceptions
exercises.instructions:
Write tests for gRPC error cases:
1. PaymentValidator service: validate amount (> 0, <= 10_000_000)
2. Test INVALID_ARGUMENT: amount = 0, amount = -100, amount = 10_000_001
3. Test NOT_FOUND: unknown merchantId
4. Verify status code AND description in each case
5. @SpringBootTest (4 tests): all error cases covered
hints: ex.getStatus().getCode(): compare to Status.Code enum
ex.getStatus().getDescription(): check specific error message
NOT_FOUND: resource doesn't exist; INVALID_ARGUMENT: bad input

exercises.title: Testing gRPC interceptors
exercises.instructions:
Test that auth interceptor blocks unauthenticated requests:
1. Simulate interceptor application: build server with interceptor
2. Call without auth → expect UNAUTHENTICATED
3. Call with valid auth → expect success
4. Test interceptor logic directly without InProcess
5. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): no header, invalid header, valid header
hints: Test interceptor logic directly: extract the auth logic into a testable method
Metadata: use real Metadata object in tests (easy to construct)
AuthCheckResult: sealed-like result type for clean assertions

exercises.title: Testing streaming RPC with TestStreamObserver
exercises.instructions:
Test a server-streaming RPC using TestStreamObserver:
1. PaymentStatusStreamer: sends 3 updates (INITIATED, PROCESSING, CONFIRMED)
2. Use TestStreamObserver to capture all messages
3. Await completion, assert all 3 statuses received
4. Test error case: invalid paymentId → onError called
5. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): happy stream, error stream
hints: isCompleted() vs hasError(): mutually exclusive — only one fires
awaitCompletion() even after error: latch released on both onCompleted and onError
assertEquals(0, values.size()): error before any onNext → 0 messages

exercises.title: Testing bidirectional streaming
exercises.instructions:
Test a bidirectional streaming RPC:
1. EchoStreamService: streams back each received message with 'ECHO:' prefix
2. Client sends 3 messages, receives 3 echoes
3. Test with TestStreamObserver and manual request sending
4. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): echoes received in order, stream completes
hints: bidiEcho returns StreamObserver<String>: the request observer (client → server)
responseObserver: the response side (server → client)
onCompleted on request: signals no more messages from client

exercises.title: gRPC test with Testcontainers for integration
exercises.instructions:
Document gRPC integration test with real database:
1. @SpringBootTest with @Container PostgreSQL
2. PaymentService writes to DB, gRPC call verifies DB state
3. @DynamicPropertySource: wire DB URL to service
4. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): payment created in DB, retrieved via gRPC
hints: Testcontainers + InProcess: real DB + in-JVM gRPC — best of both worlds
@DynamicPropertySource: wire DB URL from container to Spring datasource
Same DB that production service uses — catches schema mismatch bugs

exercises.title: Diagnose gRPC test failures
exercises.expected_solution:
Test 1: assertEquals(3, obs.getValues().size()) fails with 0
  BUG: No awaitCompletion() before assertion
  With directExecutor: streaming may still be async
  FIX: Add obs.awaitCompletion() between stream call and assertion
  Correct:
    streamer.stream('PAY-001', obs);
    obs.awaitCompletion();  // WAIT for stream to finish
    assertEquals(3, obs.getValues().size());

Test 2: no exception thrown for invalid input
  BUG: assertThrows(Exception.class) — too broad OR service doesn't throw StatusRuntimeException
  FIX 1: Use specific exception: assertThrows(StatusRuntimeException.class, () -> ...)
  FIX 2: Verify service ACTUALLY throws — check service implementation
  FIX 3: If using blocking stub: StatusRuntimeException IS thrown — maybe different method?
  Correct:
    assertThrows(StatusRuntimeException.class, () -> stub.createPayment(invalid));
    // AND verify status code:
    var ex = assertThrows(StatusRuntimeException.class, () -> ...);
    assertEquals(Status.Code.INVALID_ARGUMENT, ex.getStatus().getCode());

Test 3: server not found error
  BUG: Channel uses 'test-server' but server was registered with DIFFERENT name
  InProcessServerBuilder.generateName() generates unique name — must match!
  FIX: use same name for both builder and channel
  Correct:
    String name = InProcessServerBuilder.generateName(); // generate once
    server = InProcessServerBuilder.forName(name)...build();
    channel = InProcessChannelBuilder.forName(name)...build(); // SAME name

Test 4: obs.getError() is non-null after awaitCompletion
  BUG: Stream ended with error (onError called) but test expects null error
  Two possibilities:
    a) Service has a bug — fix the service
    b) Test is testing the wrong thing — should check for error
  FIX if service bug: debug why onError is called (check service logic)
  FIX if intentional error: change assertion
    assertNotNull(obs.getError());
    assertTrue(obs.getError() instanceof StatusRuntimeException);
    assertEquals(Status.Code.XXX, ((StatusRuntimeException)obs.getError()).getStatus().getCode());

test_cases.expected:
Test 1: assertEquals(3, obs.getValues().size()) fails with 0
  BUG: No awaitCompletion() before assertion
  With directExecutor: streaming may still be async
  FIX: Add obs.awaitCompletion() between stream call and assertion
  Correct:
    streamer.stream('PAY-001', obs);
    obs.awaitCompletion();  // WAIT for stream to finish
    assertEquals(3, obs.getValues().size());

Test 2: no exception thrown for invalid input
  BUG: assertThrows(Exception.class) — too broad OR service doesn't throw StatusRuntimeException
  FIX 1: Use specific exception: assertThrows(StatusRuntimeException.class, () -> ...)
  FIX 2: Verify service ACTUALLY throws — check service implementation
  FIX 3: If using blocking stub: StatusRuntimeException IS thrown — maybe different method?
  Correct:
    assertThrows(StatusRuntimeException.class, () -> stub.createPayment(invalid));
    // AND verify status code:
    var ex = assertThrows(StatusRuntimeException.class, () -> ...);
    assertEquals(Status.Code.INVALID_ARGUMENT, ex.getStatus().getCode());

Test 3: server not found error
  BUG: Channel uses 'test-server' but server was registered with DIFFERENT name
  InProcessServerBuilder.generateName() generates unique name — must match!
  FIX: use same name for both builder and channel
  Correct:
    String name = InProcessServerBuilder.generateName(); // generate once
    server = InProcessServerBuilder.forName(name)...build();
    channel = InProcessChannelBuilder.forName(name)...build(); // SAME name

Test 4: obs.getError() is non-null after awaitCompletion
  BUG: Stream ended with error (onError called) but test expects null error
  Two possibilities:
    a) Service has a bug — fix the service
    b) Test is testing the wrong thing — should check for error
  FIX if service bug: debug why onError is called (check service logic)
  FIX if intentional error: change assertion
    assertNotNull(obs.getError());
    assertTrue(obs.getError() instanceof StatusRuntimeException);
    assertEquals(Status.Code.XXX, ((StatusRuntimeException)obs.getError()).getStatus().getCode());

hints: Most common bug: missing awaitCompletion() before asserting stream values
Server name mismatch: generateName() must be used for both server and channel
assertThrows(StatusRuntimeException.class): always use specific type, not generic Exception

exercises.title: Complete InProcess gRPC test suite
exercises.instructions:
Build complete InProcess test suite for PaymentService:
1. PaymentServiceImpl: createPayment (unary), streamStatus (server-streaming)
2. InProcessServer setup/teardown in @BeforeEach/@AfterEach
3. Test: createPayment happy path
4. Test: createPayment invalid amount → INVALID_ARGUMENT
5. Test: streamStatus → 3 updates, all received
6. @SpringBootTest equivalent (3 tests): all pass
hints: paymentId starts with 'PAY-MRC-001': verify merchant ID embedded in payment ID
awaitCompletion before size assertion: always
List.of: compare entire list in one assertion

exercises.title: Parameterized gRPC tests
exercises.instructions:
Write parameterized gRPC tests for PaymentValidator:
1. @ParameterizedTest + @MethodSource for invalid amounts
2. Test all boundary values: 0, -1, 10_000_001
3. Test valid amounts: 1, 5000, 10_000_000
4. Each invalid amount → INVALID_ARGUMENT
5. Each valid amount → no exception
hints: @ParameterizedTest + @MethodSource: runs test for each value in stream
name = '..{0}': includes the parameter value in the test name for clarity
6 invalid + 6 valid = 12 total parameterized tests

exercises.title: Complete Razorpay gRPC test suite
exercises.instructions:
Build complete Razorpay gRPC test suite:
1. PaymentService: createPayment (unary), streamConfirmations (server-stream)
2. AuthInterceptorHarness: test UNAUTHENTICATED and valid paths
3. TestStreamObserver for streaming tests
4. @SpringBootTest (4 tests): create happy, create invalid, stream 3 updates, auth blocked
hints: awaitCompletion after direct service call: synchronous with directExecutor-style
paymentId.startsWith('PAY-MRC-001'): merchantId embedded in ID
4 tests cover: happy unary, error unary, streaming, auth

exercises.title: gRPC test with mock dependencies
exercises.instructions:
Test gRPC service with mocked dependencies:
1. PaymentGrpcService depends on PaymentRepository (JPA)
2. Mock the repository with Mockito
3. Test: payment saved to repo, response contains correct ID
4. Test: repo throws → service returns INTERNAL status
5. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): mock repo, error propagation
hints: @Mock + @ExtendWith(MockitoExtension): mock repo without Spring context
Status.INTERNAL: wrap unexpected exceptions (never let raw Exception escape gRPC)
verify(repo).save(): confirm the service called the repo with correct args

exercises.title: Complete M28 T5 Testing gRPC synthesis
exercises.instructions:
Build complete M28 T5 synthesis:
1. SimpleService: echo (unary), numbers (server-stream)
2. TestStreamObserver for streaming
3. Parameterized test for invalid inputs
4. Auth harness: test UNAUTHENTICATED path
5. @SpringBootTest (4 tests + 3 parameterized): all pass
exercises.starter_code: // Complete M28 T5 Testing gRPC synthesis
hints: 4 regular + 3 parameterized (null,empty,blank) = 7 total tests
TestStreamObserver.awaitCompletion: required even for synchronous service impl
List.of(1,2,3): compare entire list in one assertion
